text:
There was once a little old lady named Mildred who lived by a lake. She'd walk along the shore every day, watching the ducks quack merrily. One day, curiosity got the better of her. She decided to hop right into the water.

The ducks glided past, unfazed by the old lady. As she paddled further, the water wasn't cold at all—it seemed quite peaceful. She remained cautiously optimistic until she realized how far down she’d gone. With a small squeak, she popped back onto the bank.

Looking down, she wasn't horrified. She found herself admiring those webbed feet. She wondered what ducks could possibly wear such stylish boots. That evening, she wore one of those duck boots everywhere. People thought it was quite fetching. They even made her an honorary duck.

One might imagine Mildred as just such an eccentric character with a thing for wildlife. She'd go on duck-walking tours at the lake, teaching everyone how to
